analysis: Principled conformal under donor covariate shift: naive-LODO vs Mondrian + weighted covariate-shift conformal
author: trustlayer iter5 methodological-extension track
base_predictor: HistGradientBoostingRegressor (same hyperparams as conformal.py), fit on calibration rows only, per fold.
diagnostics_prereg: domain-classifier AUC (calib-vs-test) per fold: magnitude of the covariate shift.
per-condition coverage for Mondrian: does stratification tighten conditional coverage?
Y|X-invariance indirect check: if naive per-fold coverage is already near nominal, covariate shift is mild.

exchangeability_assumptions_stated.mondrian_by_condition: Requires exchangeability WITHIN each condition stratum across the cal/test donor split. Weaker per-stratum requirement; still assumes donor does not shift the residual distribution within a condition.
exchangeability_assumptions_stated.naive_LODO: Requires cal u test exchangeable. VIOLATED by donor-blocking (different donor composition). => no valid guarantee, only empirical coverage.
exchangeability_assumptions_stated.weighted_covariate_shift: Requires COVARIATE SHIFT ONLY: P_test(Y|X)=P_cal(Y|X), the shift is confined to the X-marginal, and the density ratio w(x) is (approximately) correct. Under this assumption weighted split-conformal restores VALID marginal coverage (Tibshirani et al. 2019, Thm 2).

folds: 4 leave-one-donor-out folds over donors [CE0006864, CE0008162, CE0008678, CE0010866].
honesty: Null / no-improvement is an acceptable and expected outcome. No tuning of weights or strata to hit nominal. Weights estimated once from frozen covariates; no post-hoc selection.
motivation: Naive donor-blocked LODO split-conformal breaks exchangeability BY DESIGN: calibration = donor-pairs NOT containing held-out donor d; test = donor-pairs containing d. Calibration and test are therefore drawn from different donor compositions, so standard split-conformal's exchangeability precondition (cal u test i.i.d./exchangeable) does not hold. The reported coverage is an EMPIRICAL observation, not a guaranteed one.

success_criteria_PREREG.failed: principled variant degrades empirical coverage or its assumption is demonstrably violated (e.g. Y|X shifts across donors).
success_criteria_PREREG.genuine_improvement: principled variant delivers empirical coverage AT LEAST AS CLOSE to nominal as naive at all 3 levels AND rests on a defensible (weaker/valid) guarantee. If empirical coverage is comparable but the guarantee is strictly more defensible -> report as 'marginal' (guarantee-level win, empirical tie).
success_criteria_PREREG.no_naive_was_fine: naive already achieves near-nominal coverage AND the domain shift is negligible (domain-classifier AUC ~0.5), so no principled correction is needed empirically.

target_definition: y = log1p( L2norm( DE zscore over trans genes, on-target excluded ) ), recomputed per pair — IDENTICAL to conformal.py build_pair_table.

variants_prereg.mondrian_by_condition: Class-conditional (Mondrian) split-conformal: separate calibration quantile per culture_condition stratum in {Rest,Stim8hr,Stim48hr}; each test row uses its own condition's quantile.
variants_prereg.naive_LODO: Unweighted split-conformal quantile of |residual| on calibration pairs; single q per fold/level (reproduces committed conformal_lodo_receipt.json).
variants_prereg.weighted_covariate_shift: Tibshirani et al. 2019 weighted split-conformal. Density ratio w(x)=p_test(x)/p_cal(x) estimated by a domain classifier (calib=0 vs test=1) on covariates X=[log_baseMean, condition one-hot]. Per-test-point weighted quantile of calibration |residual| with the +inf point-mass from the test weight.
